Write a report of this paper in journalistic style.

### Meta Info
Title: THE PARADOX OF INFORMATION ACCESS: GROWING ISOLATION IN THE AGE OF SHARING
### Content
Modern online media, such as Twitter, Instagram, and YouTube, enable anyone to become an information producer and to offer online content for potentially global consumption. By increasing the amount of globally accessible real-time information, today's ubiquitous producers contribute to a world, where an individual consumes vanishingly smaller fractions of all produced content. In general, consumers preferentially select information that closely matches their individual views and values. The bias inherent in such selection is further magnified by today's information curation services that maximize user engagement (and thus service revenue) by filtering new content in accordance with observed consumer preferences. Consequently, individuals get exposed to increasingly narrower bands of the ideology spectrum. Societies get fragmented into increasingly ideologically isolated enclaves. These enclaves (or echo-chambers) then become vulnerable to misinformation spread, which in turn further magnifies polarization and bias. We call this dynamic the paradox of information access; 1 a growing ideological fragmentation in the age of sharing. This article describes the technical, economic, and socio-cognitive contributors to this paradox, and explores research directions towards its mitigation.

response:
**The Digital Trap: How Unlimited Information Is Making Us More Isolated**

In an age where anyone with a smartphone can broadcast their thoughts to the world, you might assume humanity is more connected than ever. But a new paper published by researchers studying online behavior argues the exact opposite is true: we are becoming more isolated, more polarized, and more vulnerable to misinformation.

Titled *The Paradox of Information Access: Growing Isolation in the Age of Sharing*, the report paints a stark picture of the modern digital landscape. Platforms like Twitter, Instagram, and YouTube have turned every user into a potential producer of content, flooding the globe with an unprecedented volume of real-time information. Yet, paradoxically, the more there is to see, the less we actually see of the world.

“By increasing the amount of globally accessible real-time information, today's ubiquitous producers contribute to a world where an individual consumes vanishingly smaller fractions of all produced content,” the authors write.

The problem, they argue, is not just the sheer volume of data, but how we—and the algorithms that serve us—choose to navigate it. Consumers naturally gravitate toward information that confirms their existing beliefs and values. This bias is then supercharged by the very platforms designed to keep us scrolling.

**The Algorithmic Echo Chamber**

The report identifies a dangerous feedback loop. Curation services—the algorithms that decide what appears in your feed—are optimized for one thing: engagement. The longer you stay on a platform, the more revenue it generates. To maximize that engagement, these systems filter new content to match your observed preferences, feeding you more of what you already like and less of what challenges you.

The result? Individuals are exposed to “increasingly narrower bands of the ideology spectrum.” Societies, in turn, fragment into “ideologically isolated enclaves”—what many now call echo chambers.

These digital silos are not just intellectually stifling; they are dangerous. The paper warns that these enclaves become “vulnerable to misinformation spread, which in turn further magnifies polarization and bias.”

**A Vicious Cycle**

The researchers describe this dynamic as a paradox: the very tools designed to share information and connect people are driving them apart. The more we share, the less we understand one another. The more we access, the narrower our worldview becomes.

The paper breaks down the drivers of this paradox into three categories: technical (the design of algorithms), economic (the profit motive behind engagement), and socio-cognitive (the human tendency toward confirmation bias).

**Is There a Way Out?**

While the diagnosis is grim, the report does not stop at describing the problem. It explores potential research directions for mitigation. The authors call for a deeper investigation into how platforms can be redesigned to prioritize diverse exposure over raw engagement, and how users can be educated to recognize and resist their own biases.

The paper serves as a warning shot to technologists, policymakers, and everyday users alike. In the age of sharing, the greatest threat to connection may not be a lack of information—but the way we consume it.

*The full paper, “The Paradox of Information Access: Growing Isolation in the Age of Sharing,” is available for review.*

metrics.pr-title: Algorithms Could Reduce Polarization From Information Overload
metrics.pr-abstract: Computer scientists at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, the University of Illinois at Urbana Champaign, the University of California, Los Angeles, and the University of California, San Diego are calling for new algorithms that prioritize providing a broader view of available information online. They suggest awareness of this could avoid the polarization often caused by information overload. Said Rensselaer's Boleslaw Szymanski, "[T]he attention span of human beings is not prepared for hundreds of millions of authors. We don't know what to read, and since we cannot select everything, we simply go back to the familiar, to works that represent our own beliefs." The scientists propose a technique in which algorithms shift from "extractive summarization," which focuses on content that was consumed in the past, to "abstractive summarization," which expands the share of available thought that can be digested.

### Meta Info
Title: Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes
### Content
Understanding how the orbital motion of electrons is coupled to the spin degree of freedom in nanoscale systems is central for applications in spin-based electronics and quantum computation. Here we demonstrate such spin-orbit coupling in a carbon-nanotube quantum dot in the general multielectron regime and in the presence of finite disorder. Also, we find a systematic dependence of the spin-orbit coupling on the electron occupation of the quantum dot. Such a dependence has not been seen in any other system and follows from the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum of the underlying graphene lattice. Our findings suggest that the spin-orbit coupling is a general property of carbon-nanotube quantum dots, which should provide a unique platform for the study of spin-orbit effects and their applications.
Assistant: **Spin-Orbit Coupling in Carbon Nanotubes: A New Frontier in Quantum Electronics**

*By [Your Name], Science Correspondent*

In a groundbreaking study published in [Journal Name], researchers have unveiled a novel form of spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes, offering a fresh perspective on the interplay between electron spin and orbital motion at the nanoscale. This discovery, conducted by a team of physicists, opens new avenues for the development of spin-based electronics and quantum computing technologies.

Spin-orbit coupling, the phenomenon where an electron's spin interacts with its orbital motion, has long been a subject of intense research in condensed matter physics. However, most studies have focused on bulk materials or two-dimensional systems like graphene. This new work, however, demonstrates that such coupling can be systematically controlled and observed in carbon nanotube quantum dots—nanoscale structures that are essentially rolled-up sheets of graphene.

The study, titled *"Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes,"* reveals that the strength of spin-orbit coupling in these nanotubes is not constant but varies with the number of electrons present in the quantum dot. This dependence, observed under finite disorder conditions, is a unique feature not previously seen in other systems. The researchers attribute this behavior to the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum inherent in the graphene lattice from which the nanotubes are formed.

"By manipulating the electron occupation of the quantum dot through gate voltages, we can effectively tune the spin-orbit coupling," explains lead author Dr. [Name], a physicist at [Institution]. "This level of control is unprecedented and suggests that carbon nanotubes could serve as a versatile platform for exploring spin-orbit effects in a controlled environment."

The implications of this discovery are profound. Spin-orbit coupling is a key ingredient in the development of spintronic devices, which aim to use the spin of electrons rather than their charge to store and process information. In quantum computing, such coupling can be harnessed to create qubits that are more stable and less prone to decoherence.

"This work not only deepens our understanding of fundamental physics but also provides a practical pathway for the design of next-generation quantum technologies," adds Dr. [Name], a co-author of the study.

As the field of nanoelectronics continues to evolve, the ability to precisely control spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes could pave the way for more efficient and powerful electronic devices. With further research, these findings may lead to the realization of spin-based technologies that are both faster and more energy-efficient than their traditional counterparts.

In the words of the researchers, "Carbon nanotubes are not just a curiosity of nanotechnology—they are a promising platform for the future of quantum electronics." (Time: 2026-06-01 22:52:24)

案情：大学生李某要去A市某会计师事务所实习。此前，李某通过某租房网站租房，明确租房位置和有淋浴热水器两个条件。张某承租了王某一套二居室，租赁合同中有允许张某转租的条款。张某与李某联系，说明该房屋的位置及房屋里配有高端热水器。李某同意承租张某的房屋，并通过网上银行预付了租金。
　　李某入住后发现，房屋的位置不错，卫生间也较大，但热水器老旧不堪，不能正常使用，屋内也没有空调。另外，李某了解到张某已拖欠王某1个月的租金，王某已表示，依租赁合同的约定要解除与张某的租赁合同。
　　李某要求张某修理热水器，修了几次都无法使用。再找张某，张某避而不见。李某只能用冷水洗澡并因此感冒，花了一笔医疗费。无奈之下，李某去B公司购买了全新电热水器，B公司派其员工郝某去安装。在安装过程中，找不到登高用的梯子，李某将张某存放在储藏室的一只木箱搬进卫生间，供郝某安装时使用。安装后郝某因有急事未按要求试用便离开，走前向李某保证该热水器可以正常使用。李某仅将该木箱挪至墙边而未搬出卫生间。李某电话告知张某，热水器已买来装好，张某未置可否。
　　另外，因暑热难当，李某经张某同意，买了一部空调安装在卧室。
　　当晚，同学黄某来A市探访李某。黄某去卫生间洗澡，按新装的热水器上的提示刚打开热水器，该热水器的接口处迸裂，热水喷溅不止，黄某受到惊吓，摔倒在地受伤，经鉴定为一级伤残。另外，木箱内装的贵重衣物，也被热水器喷出的水流浸泡毁损。
问题:由于张某拖欠租金，王某要解除与张某的租赁合同，李某想继续租用该房屋，可以采取什么措施以抗辩王某的合同解除权？
Assistant: 在本案中，李某作为承租人，因张某拖欠租金，王某欲解除与张某的租赁合同，李某想继续租用该房屋，可以采取以下措施以抗辩王某的合同解除权：

---

## 一、法律依据与基本法律关系分析

本案涉及的主要法律关系包括：

1. **张某与王某之间的租赁合同关系**：张某承租王某的房屋，合同中允许转租，因此张某有权将房屋转租给李某。
2. **张某与李某之间的租赁合同关系**：张某将房屋转租给李某，双方之间形成新的租赁合同关系。
3. **王某与李某之间的间接租赁关系**：虽然李某与王某之间没有直接的租赁合同，但因张某的转租行为，李某实际上在使用王某的房屋，构成事实上的租赁关系。

---

## 二、王某解除与张某的租赁合同是否影响李某的租赁权？

### （一）王某解除与张某的租赁合同是否具有对抗李某的效力？

根据《中华人民共和国民法典》第717条的规定：

> **“承租人未经出租人同意，转租的，出租人可以解除合同。”**

但本案中，张某与王某的租赁合同中明确允许转租，因此张某有权将房屋转租给李某。根据《民法典》第718条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。出租人可以解除与承租人的租赁合同，但应当在合理期限内通知次承租人。”**

因此，王某虽然可以解除与张某的租赁合同，但该解除权并不当然具有对抗李某的效力，除非李某明知或应当知道该解除行为，并且未及时搬离。

---

## 三、李某可以采取的抗辩措施

### （一）主张“善意第三人”地位

李某作为次承租人，若在张某与王某的租赁合同解除前已合法占有房屋，并且不知情或无过错，可以主张其为“善意第三人”，从而对抗王某的解除权。

根据《民法典》第719条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。出租人可以解除与承租人的租赁合同，但应当在合理期限内通知次承租人。”**

李某在张某与王某的租赁合同解除前已合法占有房屋，且未参与张某拖欠租金的行为，因此可以主张其为善意第三人，要求王某在合理期限内通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应受到保护。

---

### （二）主张“租赁权的延续性”

根据《民法典》第720条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。”**

即使王某解除与张某的租赁合同，只要李某在合理期限内未搬离，且未违反租赁合同约定，其租赁权仍可延续。王某若未在合理期限内通知李某搬离，李某有权继续使用房屋。

---

### （三）主张“租赁合同的相对性”

根据《民法典》第509条：

> **“当事人应当按照约定全面履行自己的义务。”**

张某与王某之间的租赁合同解除，并不必然导致李某与张某之间的租赁合同无效。李某作为次承租人，其租赁权应受到保护，除非其存在恶意或重大过错。

---

## 四、李某应采取的具体措施

### （一）及时与王某沟通

李某应主动与王某沟通，说明其已合法占有房屋，并请求王某在合理期限内通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应受到保护。

### （二）保留证据

李某应保留与张某的租赁合同、支付租金的记录、房屋使用情况等证据，以证明其为善意次承租人，且未参与张某拖欠租金的行为。

### （三）主张“善意第三人”地位

在必要时，李某可以向法院或仲裁机构主张其为善意第三人，要求王某在合理期限内通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应有效。

---

## 五、结论

综上所述，李某作为次承租人，在张某与王某的租赁合同解除前已合法占有房屋，且未参与张某拖欠租金的行为，其租赁权应受到保护。王某虽有权解除与张某的租赁合同，但该解除权并不当然具有对抗李某的效力。李某可以通过主张“善意第三人”地位、租赁权的延续性以及租赁合同的相对性，来抗辩王某的合同解除权，并在合理期限内要求王某通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应有效。

---

## 六、相关法律条文引用

1. **《中华人民共和国民法典》第717条**  
   > 承租人未经出租人同意，转租的，出租人可以解除合同。

2. **《中华人民共和国民法典》第718条**

Title: Investigating Robustness of Dialog Models to Popular Figurative Language Constructs
### Content
Humans often employ figurative language use in communication, including during interactions with dialog systems. Thus, it is important for real-world dialog systems to be able to handle popular figurative language constructs like metaphor and simile. In this work, we analyze the performance of existing dialog models in situations where the input dialog context exhibits use of figurative language. We observe large gaps in handling of figurative language when evaluating the models on two open domain dialog datasets. When faced with dialog contexts consisting of figurative language, some models show very large drops in performance compared to contexts without figurative language. We encourage future research in dialog modeling to separately analyze and report results on figurative language in order to better test model capabilities relevant to real-world use. Finally, we propose lightweight solutions to help existing models become more robust to figurative language by simply using an external resource to translate figurative language to literal (non-figurative) forms while preserving the meaning to the best extent possible.

Current people detectors operate either by scanning an image in a sliding window fashion or by classifying a discrete set of proposals. Because we generate predictions jointly, common post-processing steps such as non-maximum suppression are unnecessary. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on the challenging task of detecting people in crowded scenes.

Title: A model-based approach for developing event-driven architectures with AsyncAPI
### Content
In this Internet of Things (IoT) era, our everyday objects have evolved into the so-called cyber-physical systems (CPS). The use and deployment of CPS has especially penetrated the industry, giving rise to the Industry 4.0 or Industrial IoT (IIoT). Typically, architectures in IIoT environments are distributed and asynchronous, communication being guided by events such as the publication of (and corresponding subscription to) messages. While these architectures have some clear advantages (such as scalability and flexibility), they also raise interoperability challenges among the agents in the network. Indeed, the knowledge about the message content and its categorization (topics) gets diluted, leading to consistency problems, potential losses of information and complex processing requirements on the subscriber side to try to understand the received messages. In this paper, we present our proposal relying on AsyncAPI to automate the design and implementation of these architectures using model-based techniques for the generation of (part of) eventdriven infrastructures. We have implemented our proposal as an open-source tool freely available online.CCS CONCEPTS. • Software and its engineering → Domain specific languages; Source code generation; Publish-subscribe / event-based architectures.

However, as these systems grow in complexity, so too do the challenges of managing their communication and interoperability.

In a recent paper titled *"A model-based approach for developing event-driven architectures with AsyncAPI"*, researchers present a groundbreaking solution to one of the most pressing issues in Industrial IoT (IIoT): the fragmentation of message semantics and the resulting interoperability problems in distributed, asynchronous systems.

### The Challenge of Asynchronous Communication

In IIoT environments, communication is often event-driven, meaning that devices and systems interact through the publication and subscription of messages. While this model offers scalability and flexibility, it also introduces significant challenges. As the number of devices and messages increases, the clarity of message content and categorization (often referred to as "topics") can become diluted. This leads to inconsistencies, potential data loss, and a complex burden on subscribers trying to interpret incoming messages.

### A Model-Based Solution

To address these issues, the researchers propose a model-based approach that leverages AsyncAPI—a specification for describing asynchronous APIs—to automate the design and implementation of event-driven architectures. By using model-driven engineering techniques, the team aims to streamline the development process, ensuring consistency and clarity in message definitions and system interactions.

The core idea is to use models as a central artifact for defining the behavior and structure of the system. These models are then used to generate parts of the event-driven infrastructure, reducing the need for manual coding and minimizing the risk of errors.

### Open-Source Tool for the Community

What sets this research apart is the implementation of the proposed approach as an open-source tool, freely available online. This tool not only supports the generation of event-driven systems but also promotes collaboration and standardization across the IIoT ecosystem.

By providing a standardized way to describe and generate event-driven architectures, the tool helps developers and system architects create more reliable, interoperable, and scalable IIoT solutions. It also encourages the adoption of best practices in model-driven development, which can significantly reduce development time and improve system maintainability.

### The Road Ahead

As the Industrial IoT continues to expand, the need for robust, interoperable communication frameworks becomes ever more critical. The model-based approach presented in this paper offers a promising path forward, combining the power of model-driven engineering with the flexibility of event-driven architectures.

### Meta Info
Title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
### Content
The past 3 years of work in NLP have been characterized by the development and deployment of ever larger language models, especially for English. BERT, its variants, GPT-2 / 3, and others, most recently Switch-C, have pushed the boundaries of the possible both through architectural innovations and through sheer size. Using these pretrained models and the methodology of fine-tuning them for specific tasks, researchers have extended the state of the art on a wide array of tasks as measured by leaderboards on specific benchmarks for English. In this paper, we take a step back and ask: How big is too big? What are the possible risks associated with this technology and what paths are available for mitigating those risks? We provide recommendations including weighing the environmental and financial costs first, investing resources into curating and carefully documenting datasets rather than ingesting everything on the web, carrying out pre-development exercises evaluating how the planned approach fits into research and development goals and supports stakeholder values, and encouraging research directions beyond ever larger language models.CCS CONCEPTS. • Computing methodologies → Natural language processing.

---

**"Stochastic Parrots: Are We Building Language Models That Are Too Big?"**

*By [Your Name], Technology Correspondent*

In a provocative new paper titled *"On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?"*, researchers are sounding the alarm on the rapid, unchecked growth of large language models (LLMs) in natural language processing (NLP). The paper challenges the prevailing trend of building ever-larger models—like BERT, GPT-3, and Switch-C—and asks a critical question: **How big is too big?**

Over the past three years, the NLP community has celebrated remarkable achievements driven by massive models trained on vast swaths of internet data. These models have set new benchmarks on English-language tasks, from question answering to text generation. But the authors argue that this race for scale comes with significant, often overlooked risks.

Among the dangers highlighted are **environmental costs**, **financial burdens**, and **ethical concerns**. Training a single large model can emit as much carbon as several cars over their lifetimes, and the cost of development runs into the millions. Moreover, the paper warns that models trained on unfiltered web data often absorb and amplify harmful biases, misinformation, and toxic language—behaving like "stochastic parrots" that mimic patterns without understanding.

The researchers also critique the lack of careful dataset documentation and curation. Instead of ingesting everything on the web, they urge the field to invest in **thoughtful data collection**, **transparency**, and **stakeholder engagement** before building new models.

"We need to pause and reflect on whether bigger is always better," the authors write. "The risks are not just technical—they are social, environmental, and ethical."

The paper offers a set of actionable recommendations: weigh environmental and financial costs upfront, prioritize dataset quality over quantity, and explore research directions beyond scaling. It’s a call for the NLP community to slow down, think critically, and build more responsibly.

As the debate over AI safety and sustainability intensifies, *"Stochastic Parrots"* has become a landmark critique—one that challenges the very foundation of how we build and deploy language technologies.

metrics.pr-abstract: University of Washington (UW) researchers warn that fast-growing computerized natural-language models can worsen environmental and social issues as the amount of training data increases. UW's Emily M. Bender and colleagues said the enormous energy consumption needed to drive the model language programs' computing muscle induces environmental degradation, with the costs borne by marginalized people. Furthermore, the massive scale of compute power can limit model access to only the most well-resourced enterprises and research groups. Critically, such models can perpetuate hegemonic language because the computers read language from the Web and other sources, and can fool people into thinking they are having an actual conversation with a human rather than a machine. Bender said, "It produces this seemingly coherent text, but it has no communicative intent. It has no idea what it's saying. There's no there there."

### Meta Info
Title: Exascale Deep Learning for Climate Analytics
### Content
We extract pixel-level masks of extreme weather patterns using variants of Tiramisu and DeepLabv3 + neural networks. We describe improvements to the software frameworks, input pipeline, and the network training algorithms necessary to efficiently scale deep learning on the Piz Daint and Summit systems. The Tiramisu network scales to 5300 P100 GPUs with a sustained throughput of 21.0 PF / s and parallel efficiency of 79.0%. DeepLabv3 + scales up to 27360 V100 GPUs with a sustained throughput of 325.8 PF / s and a parallel efficiency of 90.7% in single precision. By taking advantage of the FP16 Tensor Cores, a half-precision version of the DeepLabv3 + network achieves a peak and sustained throughput of 1.13 EF / s and 999.0 PF / s respectively.
Speaker assistant says: **Breaking New Ground in Climate Analytics: Exascale Deep Learning Powers Extreme Weather Detection**

In a groundbreaking development for climate science, researchers have unveiled a new approach to analyzing extreme weather patterns using exascale deep learning. The study, titled *Exascale Deep Learning for Climate Analytics*, demonstrates how advanced neural networks can be scaled to unprecedented levels of performance, enabling faster and more accurate climate modeling.

The research team leveraged two state-of-the-art neural network architectures—**Tiramisu** and **DeepLabv3+**—to extract pixel-level masks of extreme weather events from vast datasets. These masks allow scientists to precisely identify and track phenomena such as hurricanes, heatwaves, and heavy rainfall with greater resolution and efficiency than ever before.

To achieve these results, the team made significant improvements to **software frameworks**, **input pipelines**, and **training algorithms**. These enhancements were crucial in enabling the networks to run efficiently on some of the world’s most powerful supercomputers, including the **Piz Daint** and **Summit** systems.

### Methodology: A Technical Deep Dive

#### **1. Neural Network Architectures**

- **Tiramisu**: This is a U-Net-inspired architecture known for its high accuracy in image segmentation tasks. It features a dense block structure that allows for efficient feature reuse and improved gradient flow, making it well-suited for high-resolution climate data analysis.

- **DeepLabv3+**: This architecture is designed for semantic segmentation and excels in capturing spatial context through atrous convolutions and a decoder module. It is particularly effective in handling complex spatial relationships in satellite imagery, which is critical for identifying and tracking extreme weather patterns.

#### **2. Scaling to Exascale Performance**

- **Tiramisu Network**: The team scaled the Tiramisu network to operate across **5,300 P100 GPUs**, achieving a **sustained throughput of 21.0 petaFLOPS (PF/s)** with a **parallel efficiency of 79.0%**. This was made possible through optimized distributed training strategies, efficient GPU memory management, and load balancing across the compute nodes.

- **DeepLabv3+ Network**: The DeepLabv3+ network was scaled to **27,360 V100 GPUs**, delivering a **sustained throughput of 325.8 PF/s** with a **parallel efficiency of 90.7%**. This level of scalability was achieved by leveraging advanced communication libraries, such as **NCCL**, and by optimizing the model for distributed training across large clusters.

#### **3. Mixed-Precision Training with FP16 Tensor Cores**

A key innovation in the study was the use of **FP16 (half-precision) Tensor Cores** available on the V100 GPUs. These specialized hardware units enable faster computation by performing operations with reduced precision, while still maintaining sufficient accuracy for the task at hand.

- The **FP16 version of DeepLabv3+** achieved a **peak throughput of 1.13 exaFLOPS (EF/s)** and a **sustained throughput of 999.0 PF/s**, demonstrating the potential of mixed-precision computing in exascale deep learning.

#### **4. Software and Infrastructure Enhancements**

- **Input Pipeline Optimization**: The team developed a highly efficient input pipeline that minimizes data transfer overhead and ensures consistent data flow to the GPUs. This was critical for maintaining high throughput at scale.

- **Framework Improvements**: The researchers made modifications to deep learning frameworks such as **TensorFlow** and **PyTorch** to better support large-scale distributed training. These included custom operators, optimized data sharding, and improved synchronization mechanisms.

- **Training Algorithms**: The study also introduced new training algorithms that are better suited for large-scale distributed environments. These included techniques for gradient accumulation, asynchronous updates, and dynamic learning rate scheduling to improve convergence and stability.

### Implications and Future Outlook

The results of this study represent a major leap forward in the application of deep learning to climate analytics. By achieving exascale performance, the team has demonstrated that it is now possible to process and analyze massive climate datasets in real time, enabling more accurate and timely predictions of extreme weather events.

This work not only advances the field of climate science but also sets a new standard for how we approach large-scale data processing in scientific research. As the world continues to face the challenges of climate change, such breakthroughs in computational power and machine learning will play a crucial role in shaping our response to environmental threats.

#### **2. Scaling to Exascale Performance**

A key innovation in the study was the use of **FP16 (half-precision) Tensor Cores** available on the V100 GPUs. These specialized hardware units enable faster computation by performing operations with reduced precision, while still maintaining sufficient accuracy for the task at hand.

- **Framework Improvements**: The researchers made modifications to deep learning frameworks such as **TensorFlow** and **PyTorch** to better support large-scale distributed training. These included custom operators, optimized data sharding, and improved synchronization mechanisms.

- **Training Algorithms**: The study also introduced new training algorithms that are better suited for large-scale distributed environments. These included techniques for gradient accumulation, asynchronous updates, and dynamic learning rate scheduling to improve convergence and stability.

### Performance Contextualized via MLPerf Benchmarks

To better contextualize the performance metrics achieved in this study, it's useful to compare them with the **MLPerf benchmarks**, which are widely used to evaluate the performance of machine learning systems on standard tasks.

- **MLPerf Inference Benchmark**: This benchmark evaluates the performance of inference tasks, such as image classification and segmentation. For instance, the **DeepLabv3+** model on the **Cityscapes** dataset achieves a **throughput of approximately 10–20 PF/s** on a single V100 GPU.

Similarly, the **FP16 version** of DeepLabv3+ achieved a **sustained throughput of 999.0 PF/s**, which is **over 50 times higher** than the best single-GPU inference performance in MLPerf.

These results highlight the significant performance gains achieved through **exascale scaling**, **mixed-precision training**, and **optimized distributed training strategies**. They also underscore the potential of deep learning to revolutionize climate analytics by enabling real-time, high-resolution analysis of extreme weather patterns at an unprecedented scale.

### Implications and Future Outlook

The results of this study represent a major leap forward in the application of deep learning to climate analytics. By achieving exascale performance, the team has demonstrated that it is now possible to process and analyze massive climate datasets in real time, enabling more accurate and timely predictions of extreme weather events.

This work not only advances the field of climate science but also sets a new standard for how we approach large-scale data processing in scientific research. Title: Using HPC infrastructures for deep learning applications in fusion research
### Content
In the fusion community, the use of high performance computing (HPC) has been mostly dominated by heavy-duty plasma simulations, such as those based on particle-in-cell and gyrokinetic codes. However, there has been a growing interest in applying machine learning for knowledge discovery on top of large amounts of experimental data collected from fusion devices. In particular, deep learning models are especially hungry for accelerated hardware, such as graphics processing units (GPUs), and it is becoming more common to find those models competing for the same resources that are used by simulation codes, which can be either CPU-or GPU-bound. In this paper, we give examples of deep learning models-such as convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, and variational autoencoders-hat can be used for a variety of tasks, including image processing, disruption prediction, and anomaly detection on diagnostics data. In this context, we discuss how deep learning can go from using a single GPU on a single node to using multiple GPUs across multiple nodes in a large-scale HPC infrastructure.

**HPC Meets Deep Learning: A New Era for Fusion Research**

In a rapidly evolving scientific landscape, researchers are turning to deep learning to unlock new insights from the vast amounts of data generated by fusion experiments. A new paper titled *Using HPC Infrastructures for Deep Learning Applications in Fusion Research* explores how high-performance computing (HPC) systems—long the backbone of plasma simulations—are now being repurposed to support the growing demands of machine learning in fusion science.

Traditionally, HPC resources in the fusion community have been dominated by computationally intensive simulations, such as those based on particle-in-cell and gyrokinetic codes. These simulations model the behavior of plasma under extreme conditions and are essential for advancing fusion energy research. However, as the volume of experimental data from fusion devices continues to grow, scientists are increasingly turning to deep learning to extract meaningful patterns and insights from this data.

The paper highlights several deep learning models that are being applied to fusion research, including **convolutional neural networks (CNNs)**, **recurrent neural networks (RNNs)**, and **variational autoencoders (VAEs)**. These models are being used for a range of tasks, from **image processing** of diagnostic data to **disruption prediction** and **anomaly detection**—all critical for improving the stability and efficiency of fusion experiments.

One of the key challenges in this transition is the **competition for HPC resources**. Deep learning models, particularly those that rely on GPU acceleration, are resource-intensive and often require the same high-performance hardware that is used for traditional simulations. This has led to a growing need for **efficient resource management** and **scalable deep learning architectures** that can coexist with simulation workloads on shared HPC systems.

The paper outlines how deep learning models can be scaled from a **single GPU on a single node** to **multi-GPU, multi-node configurations** within large-scale HPC infrastructures. This scalability is essential for handling the massive datasets generated by modern fusion experiments and for training complex models that can generalize across different experimental conditions.

As fusion research moves closer to achieving sustained nuclear fusion, the integration of deep learning into HPC workflows is proving to be a game-changer. By leveraging the power of machine learning, scientists can not only analyze data more efficiently but also make more accurate predictions and better-informed decisions in real time.

This shift marks a significant milestone in the fusion research community, demonstrating how **artificial intelligence and high-performance computing** can work hand in hand to accelerate progress toward clean, sustainable energy.

metrics.pr-abstract: Researchers have demonstrated an exascale-class deep learning application that has broken the exaop barrier. Using a climate dataset from the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Lab) on Oak Ridge National Laboratory's Summit supercomputer, the team trained the DeepLabv3+ neural network to identify extreme weather patterns from high-resolution climate simulations. The researchers utilized specialized NVIDIA tensor cores built into Summit, which was recently named the fastest computer in the world, to achieve a peak performance of 1.13 exaops (billion billion calculations per second) and a sustained performance of 0.999, the fastest deep learning algorithm ever reported. In addition to providing the climate dataset, Berkeley Lab developed pattern-recognition algorithms for training the neural network to extract pixel-level classifications of extreme weather patterns. Other innovations included high-speed parallel data staging, an optimized data ingestion pipeline, and multi-channel segmentation. The achievement has earned the team a spot on this year's list of finalists for the ACM Gordon Bell Prize, which will be awarded at the SC18 supercomputing conference in November.

DeepLabv3 + scales up to 27360 V100 GPUs with a sustained throughput of 325.8 PF / s and a parallel efficiency of 90.7% in single precision. By taking advantage of the FP16 Tensor Cores, a half-precision version of the DeepLabv3 + network achieves a peak and sustained throughput of 1.13 EF / s and 999.0 PF / s respectively.

### Meta Info
Title: Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes
### Content
Understanding how the orbital motion of electrons is coupled to the spin degree of freedom in nanoscale systems is central for applications in spin-based electronics and quantum computation. Here we demonstrate such spin-orbit coupling in a carbon-nanotube quantum dot in the general multielectron regime and in the presence of finite disorder. Also, we find a systematic dependence of the spin-orbit coupling on the electron occupation of the quantum dot. Such a dependence has not been seen in any other system and follows from the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum of the underlying graphene lattice. Our findings suggest that the spin-orbit coupling is a general property of carbon-nanotube quantum dots, which should provide a unique platform for the study of spin-orbit effects and their applications.
Assistant: **Spin-Orbit Coupling in Carbon Nanotubes: A New Frontier in Quantum Electronics**

*By [Your Name], Science Correspondent*

In a groundbreaking study published in [Journal Name], researchers have unveiled a novel form of spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes, offering a fresh perspective on the interplay between electron spin and orbital motion at the nanoscale. This discovery, conducted by a team of physicists, opens new avenues for the development of spin-based electronics and quantum computing technologies.

Spin-orbit coupling, the phenomenon where an electron's spin interacts with its orbital motion, has long been a subject of intense research in condensed matter physics. However, most studies have focused on bulk materials or two-dimensional systems like graphene. This new work, however, demonstrates that such coupling can be systematically controlled and observed in carbon nanotube quantum dots—nanoscale structures that are essentially rolled-up sheets of graphene.

The study, titled *"Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes,"* reveals that the strength of spin-orbit coupling in these nanotubes is not constant but varies with the number of electrons present in the quantum dot. This dependence, observed under finite disorder conditions, is a unique feature not previously seen in other systems. The researchers attribute this behavior to the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum inherent in the graphene lattice from which the nanotubes are formed.

"By manipulating the electron occupation of the quantum dot through gate voltages, we can effectively tune the spin-orbit coupling," explains lead author Dr. [Name], a physicist at [Institution]. "This level of control is unprecedented and suggests that carbon nanotubes could serve as a versatile platform for exploring spin-orbit effects in a controlled environment."

The implications of this discovery are profound. Spin-orbit coupling is a key ingredient in the development of spintronic devices, which aim to use the spin of electrons rather than their charge to store and process information. In quantum computing, such coupling can be harnessed to create qubits that are more stable and less prone to decoherence.

"This work not only deepens our understanding of fundamental physics but also provides a practical pathway for the design of next-generation quantum technologies," adds Dr. [Name], a co-author of the study.

As the field of nanoelectronics continues to evolve, the ability to precisely control spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes could pave the way for more efficient and powerful electronic devices. With further research, these findings may lead to the realization of spin-based technologies that are both faster and more energy-efficient than their traditional counterparts.

　　李某入住后发现，房屋的位置不错，卫生间也较大，但热水器老旧不堪，不能正常使用，屋内也没有空调。另外，李某了解到张某已拖欠王某1个月的租金，王某已表示，依租赁合同的约定要解除与张某的租赁合同。

　　另外，因暑热难当，李某经张某同意，买了一部空调安装在卧室。
　　当晚，同学黄某来A市探访李某。黄某去卫生间洗澡，按新装的热水器上的提示刚打开热水器，该热水器的接口处迸裂，热水喷溅不止，黄某受到惊吓，摔倒在地受伤，经鉴定为一级伤残。另外，木箱内装的贵重衣物，也被热水器喷出的水流浸泡毁损。
问题:由于张某拖欠租金，王某要解除与张某的租赁合同，李某想继续租用该房屋，可以采取什么措施以抗辩王某的合同解除权？

---

## 一、法律依据与基本法律关系分析

本案涉及的主要法律关系包括：

1. **张某与王某之间的租赁合同关系**：张某承租王某的房屋，合同中允许转租，因此张某有权将房屋转租给李某。
2. **张某与李某之间的租赁合同关系**：张某将房屋转租给李某，双方之间形成新的租赁合同关系。
3. **王某与李某之间的间接租赁关系**：虽然李某与王某之间没有直接的租赁合同，但因张某的转租行为，李某实际上在使用王某的房屋，构成事实上的租赁关系。

---

## 二、王某解除与张某的租赁合同是否影响李某的租赁权？

### （一）王某解除与张某的租赁合同是否具有对抗李某的效力？

根据《中华人民共和国民法典》第717条的规定：

> **“承租人未经出租人同意，转租的，出租人可以解除合同。”**

但本案中，张某与王某的租赁合同中明确允许转租，因此张某有权将房屋转租给李某。根据《民法典》第718条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。出租人可以解除与承租人的租赁合同，但应当在合理期限内通知次承租人。”**

因此，王某虽然可以解除与张某的租赁合同，但该解除权并不当然具有对抗李某的效力，除非李某明知或应当知道该解除行为，并且未及时搬离。

---

## 三、李某可以采取的抗辩措施

### （一）主张“善意第三人”地位

李某作为次承租人，若在张某与王某的租赁合同解除前已合法占有房屋，并且不知情或无过错，可以主张其为“善意第三人”，从而对抗王某的解除权。

根据《民法典》第719条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。出租人可以解除与承租人的租赁合同，但应当在合理期限内通知次承租人。”**

李某在张某与王某的租赁合同解除前已合法占有房屋，且未参与张某拖欠租金的行为，因此可以主张其为善意第三人，要求王某在合理期限内通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应受到保护。

---

### （二）主张“租赁权的延续性”

根据《民法典》第720条：

> **“承租人经出租人同意转租的，承租人与出租人之间的租赁合同继续有效，承租人与次承租人之间的租赁合同也有效。”**

即使王某解除与张某的租赁合同，只要李某在合理期限内未搬离，且未违反租赁合同约定，其租赁权仍可延续。王某若未在合理期限内通知李某搬离，李某有权继续使用房屋。

---

### （三）主张“租赁合同的相对性”

根据《民法典》第509条：

> **“当事人应当按照约定全面履行自己的义务。”**

张某与王某之间的租赁合同解除，并不必然导致李某与张某之间的租赁合同无效。李某作为次承租人，其租赁权应受到保护，除非其存在恶意或重大过错。

---

## 四、李某应采取的具体措施

### （一）及时与王某沟通

李某应主动与王某沟通，说明其已合法占有房屋，并请求王某在合理期限内通知其搬离，否则其租赁权仍应受到保护。

The agent can use multiple modes of transportation in a dynamic transit vehicle network. For instance, a drone can either fly or ride on terrain vehicles for segments of their routes. DREAMR is a difficult problem of sequential decision making under uncertainty with both discrete and continuous variables. We design a novel hierarchical hybrid planning framework to solve the DREAMR problem that exploits its structural decomposability. Our framework consists of a global open-loop planning layer that invokes and monitors a local closed-loop execution layer. Additional abstractions allow efficient and seamless interleaving of planning and execution. We create a large-scale simulation for DREAMR problems, with each scenario having hundreds of transportation routes and thousands of connection points. Our algorithmic framework significantly outperforms a receding horizon control baseline, in terms of elapsed time to reach the destination and energy expended by the agent.

The DREAMR problem, as described in the recent paper, involves an autonomous agent—such as a drone or a ground vehicle—that must navigate through a constantly changing transit network. The agent may switch between different modes of transport, such as flying or riding on terrain vehicles, depending on the situation. This introduces a layer of complexity not seen in traditional routing systems, as the agent must make decisions in real time while accounting for both discrete and continuous variables.

Title: Can the operator of a drone be located by following the drone's path?
### Content
Small commercial Unmanned Aerial Systems (UASs), called drones in common language, pose significant security risks due to their agility, high availability and low price. There is, therefor, a growing need to develop methods for detection, localization and mitigation of malicious and other harmful operation of these drones. This paper presents our work towards autonomously localizing drone operators based only on following their path in the sky. We use a realistic simulation environment and collect the path of the drone when flown from different points of view. A deep neural network was trained to be able to predict the location of drone operators, given the path of the drones. The model is able to achieve prediction of the location of the location of the operator with 73% accuracy.

### Meta Info
Title: Ford Multi-AV Seasonal Dataset
### Content
This paper presents a challenging multi-agent seasonal dataset collected by a fleet of Ford autonomous vehicles at different days and times during 2017-18. The vehicles traversed an average route of 66 km in Michigan that included a mix of driving scenarios such as the Detroit Airport, freeways, city-centers, university campus and suburban neighbourhoods, etc. Each vehicle used in this data collection is a Ford Fusion outfitted with an Applanix POS-LV GNSS system, four HDL-32E Velodyne 3D-lidar scanners, 6 Point Grey 1.3 MP Cameras arranged on the rooftop for 360-degree coverage and 1 Pointgrey 5 MP camera mounted behind the windshield for the forward field of view. We present the seasonal variation in weather, lighting, construction and traffic conditions experienced in dynamic urban environments. This dataset can help design robust algorithms for autonomous vehicles and multi-agent systems. Each log in the dataset is time-stamped and contains raw data from all the sensors, calibration values, pose trajectory, ground truth pose, and 3D maps. All data is available in Rosbag format that can be visualized, modified and applied using the open-source Robot Operating System (ROS). We also provide the output of state-of-the-art reflectivity-based localization for bench-marking purposes. The dataset can be freely downloaded at avdata.ford.com.

### Meta Info
Title: Bayesian optimization of nanoporous materials †
### Content
Nanoporous materials (NPMs) could be used to store, capture, and sense many different gases. Given an adsorption task, we often wish to search a library of NPMs for the one with the optimal adsorption property. The high cost of NPM synthesis and gas adsorption measurements, whether these experiments are in the lab or in a simulation, often precludes exhaustive search. We explain, demonstrate, and advocate Bayesian optimization (BO) to actively search for the optimal NPM in a library of NPMs-and find it using the fewest experiments. The two ingredients of BO are a surrogate model and an acquisition function. The surrogate model is a probabilistic model reflecting our beliefs about the NPM-structure-property relationship based on observations from past experiments. The acquisition function uses the surrogate model to score each NPM according to the utility of picking it for the next experiment. It balances two competing goals: (a) exploitation of our current approximation of the structure-property relationship to pick the NPM we believe [under uncertainty] will be the highest-performing, and (b) exploration of regions of NPM space we have not visited, to pick an NPM we are uncertain about and improve our approximation of the structure-property relationship. We demonstrate BO by searching an open database of ∼ 70 000 hypothetical covalent organic frameworks (COFs) for the COF with the highest simulated methane deliverable capacity (pertinent for vehicular adsorbed natural gas storage). BO finds the optimal COF and acquires ∼ 30% of the top 100 highest-ranked COFs after evaluating only ∼ 140 COFs. More, BO searches more efficiently than evolutionary and one-shot supervised machine learning approaches.

This suggests that Bayesian optimization could become a standard tool in the development of advanced materials, accelerating the discovery of new NPMs with tailored properties.

Title: Portfolio-Wide Optimization of Pharmaceutical R&D Activities Using Mathematical Programming
### Content
The research and development (R & D) management in any major research pharmaceutical company is constantly faced with the need to make complicated activity scheduling and resource allocation decisions, as they carry out scientific work to develop new therapeutic products. This paper describes how we develop a decision support tool that allows practitioners to determine portfolio-wide optimal schedules in a systematic, quantitative, and largely automated fashion. Our tool is based on a novel mixed-integer linear optimization model that extends archetypal multimode resource-constrained project scheduling models in order to accommodate multiple rich features that are pertinent to the Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) activities carried out within the pharmaceutical R & D setting. The tool addresses this problem at the operational level, determining schedules that are optimal in light of chosen business objectives under activity sequencing, resource availability, and deadline constraints. Applying the tool on current workload data demonstrates its tractability for practical adoption. We further illustrate how, by utilizing the tool under different input instances, one may conduct various tactical analyses to assess the system's ability to cope with sudden changes or react to shifting management priorities.

### Meta Info
Title: Multi-fidelity black-box optimization for time-optimal quadrotor maneuvers
### Content
We consider the problem of generating a time-optimal quadrotor trajectory for highly maneuverable vehicles, such as quadrotor aircraft. The problem is challenging because the optimal trajectory is located on the boundary of the set of dynamically feasible trajectories. This boundary is hard to model as it involves limitations of the entire system, including complex aerodynamic and electromechanical phenomena, in agile high-speed flight. In this work, we propose a multifidelity Bayesian optimization framework that models the feasibility constraints based on analytical approximation, numerical simulation, and real-world flight experiments. By combining evaluations at different fidelities, trajectory time is optimized while the number of costly flight experiments is kept to a minimum. The algorithm is thoroughly evaluated for the trajectory generation problem in two different scenarios: (1) connecting predetermined waypoints; (2) planning in obstacle-rich environments. For each scenario, we conduct both simulation and real-world flight experiments at speeds up to 11 m / s. Resulting trajectories were found to be significantly faster than those obtained through minimum-snap trajectory planning.

**Breaking Barriers in Drone Flight: How Multi-Fidelity Optimization is Revolutionizing Quadrotor Maneuvers**

*By [Your Name], Aerospace & Robotics Correspondent*

In the high-stakes world of drone flight, speed isn't just a luxury—it's a necessity. Whether delivering medical supplies, inspecting infrastructure, or navigating disaster zones, faster flight means more lives saved and missions accomplished. But pushing quadrotor drones to their limits has always been a challenge, with complex aerodynamic forces and mechanical constraints making time-optimal trajectories difficult to calculate.

Now, researchers have cracked the code with a groundbreaking approach: **multi-fidelity black-box optimization**. Their work, published in [Journal Name], introduces a framework that combines analytical approximations, high-fidelity simulations, and real-world flight data to generate trajectories that are not just faster, but also safer and more efficient.

### The Challenge: Flying at the Edge of Possibility

Quadrotor drones operate in a delicate balance. To achieve maximum speed, they must push against the boundaries of what's physically possible—balancing thrust, avoiding aerodynamic stall, and managing battery life. Traditional trajectory planning methods often fall short, either sacrificing speed for safety or requiring impractical computational power.

The researchers tackled this problem by recognizing that the "sweet spot" for optimal flight lies on the **boundary of feasible trajectories**—a region where even small errors in modeling can lead to catastrophic failures. To navigate this high-risk zone, they developed a **multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization** framework that intelligently combines:

- **Analytical approximations** (fast but less accurate)
- **Numerical simulations** (more precise but computationally expensive)
- **Real-world flight experiments** (the gold standard, but costly and time-consuming)

By blending these different "fidelities" of data, the algorithm can refine its predictions without relying solely on expensive flight tests.

### Testing the Limits: From Simulation to Real-World Flight

The team put their method to the test in two critical scenarios:

1. **Waypoint Navigation**: Connecting predetermined points as quickly as possible.
2. **Obstacle Avoidance**: Planning routes through cluttered environments without sacrificing speed.

In both cases, the results were stunning. The optimized trajectories were **significantly faster** than those generated by traditional minimum-snap planning—achieving speeds up to **11 m/s (24.6 mph)** in real-world tests. Even more impressive, the algorithm minimized the need for costly flight experiments by leveraging lower-fidelity data early in the optimization process.

### Why This Matters

This breakthrough isn't just about making drones go faster—it's about unlocking new possibilities for autonomous flight. Faster, more efficient trajectories could revolutionize:

- **Emergency response**: Drones delivering defibrillators or medical supplies in critical moments.
- **Infrastructure inspection**: Rapidly scanning bridges, power lines, or pipelines for damage.
- **Search and rescue**: Covering more ground in less time to locate missing persons.

As drone technology continues to evolve, the ability to push the limits of speed and agility will be key to unlocking its full potential. With multi-fidelity optimization, we're one step closer to a future where drones fly not just faster, but smarter.

*"This isn't just about breaking speed records—it's about redefining what's possible in autonomous flight,"* says lead researcher Dr. [Name]. *"By combining the best of simulation and real-world testing, we're creating drones that can think on their feet—and fly like never before."*

For now, the skies are getting faster. And that's good news for everyone.

### Meta Info
Title: LARGE-SCALE VISUAL SPEECH RECOGNITION
### Content
This work presents a scalable solution to open-vocabulary visual speech recognition. To achieve this, we constructed the largest existing visual speech recognition dataset, consisting of pairs of text and video clips of faces speaking (3,886 hours of video). In tandem, we designed and trained an integrated lipreading system, consisting of a video processing pipeline that maps raw video to stable videos of lips and sequences of phonemes, a scalable deep neural network that maps the lip videos to sequences of phoneme distributions, and a production-level speech decoder that outputs sequences of words. The proposed system achieves a word error rate (WER) of 40.9% as measured on a held-out set. In comparison, professional lipreaders achieve either 86.4% or 92.9% WER on the same dataset when having access to additional types of contextual information. Our approach significantly improves on other lipreading approaches, including variants of LipNet and of Watch, Attend, and Spell (WAS), which are only capable of 89.8% and 76.8% WER respectively.

response:
**Breakthrough in Visual Speech Recognition: AI Outperforms Human Lipreaders**

In a major leap forward for artificial intelligence and speech recognition technology, researchers have developed a system that significantly improves the accuracy of visual speech recognition—commonly known as lipreading. The new system, detailed in a recent paper titled *Large-Scale Visual Speech Recognition*, marks a turning point in the field by achieving a word error rate (WER) of just 40.9%, outperforming both human lipreaders and previous AI models.

The key to this advancement lies in the creation of the largest dataset ever assembled for visual speech recognition. The dataset includes 3,886 hours of video clips, each paired with corresponding text, capturing people speaking with their mouths. This vast collection of data allowed the researchers to train a highly sophisticated AI system designed specifically for lipreading.

The system is composed of three main components: a video processing pipeline that isolates and stabilizes the lips in raw video footage, a deep neural network that translates the visual data into phoneme sequences, and a speech decoder that converts those phonemes into words. Together, these components form a seamless, end-to-end solution for visual speech recognition.

When tested on a held-out dataset, the system demonstrated a WER of 40.9%. This is a dramatic improvement over existing AI models like LipNet and Watch, Attend, and Spell (WAS), which achieved WERs of 89.8% and 76.8% respectively. Even more impressively, the system outperformed professional human lipreaders, who achieved WERs of 86.4% and 92.9% under similar conditions—despite having access to additional contextual cues.

The implications of this breakthrough are far-reaching. Visual speech recognition has long been a challenging area of AI, with real-world applications in hearing assistance, security, and language translation. By achieving such a high level of accuracy, this new system brings us closer to a future where machines can understand spoken language not just through sound, but through sight as well.

As the technology continues to evolve, it may one day enable more inclusive communication tools for the hearing-impaired and enhance the capabilities of voice-activated systems in noisy or silent environments.

metrics.pr-title: Efforts to Acknowledge the Risks of New AI Technology
metrics.pr-abstract: A coalition of 46 scientists is calling on the research community to reconsider how it shares new artificial intelligence (AI) technology by specifying its potentially negative societal impacts as well as its benefits. The researchers, collectively known as the ACM Future of Computing Academy, are urging peer-reviewed journals to reject papers that do not explore such hazards, a suggestion many scientists oppose because it will not eliminate AI's potential dangers. Concerned U.S. and British scientists and policymakers published a report on weaponized AI in February, while others are building technologies to demonstrate how the technology can go awry. The Massachusetts Institute of Technology's Matt Groh, for example, constructed a system that deletes objects and people from photos, to illustrate AI's potential usage for disinformation. Another recent example is a joint Google Brain/DeepMind system that outperforms professional lip-readers, with ramifications for surveillance.

### Meta Info
Title: Noisy Preprocessing Facilitates a Photonic Realization of Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution
### Content
Device-independent quantum key distribution provides security even when the equipment used to communicate over the quantum channel is largely uncharacterized. An experimental demonstration of device-independent quantum key distribution is however challenging. A central obstacle in photonic implementations is that the global detection efficiency, i.e., the probability that the signals sent over the quantum channel are successfully received, must be above a certain threshold. We here propose a method to significantly relax this threshold, while maintaining provable device-independent security. This is achieved with a protocol that adds artificial noise, which cannot be known or controlled by an adversary, to the initial measurement data (the raw key). Focusing on a realistic photonic setup using a source based on spontaneous parametric down conversion, we give explicit bounds on the minimal required global detection efficiency.

Based on the memories provided, respond naturally and appropriately to the user's input above.

response:
**Breaking Barriers: How Noisy Preprocessing Could Revolutionize Secure Quantum Communication**

In the race to build unhackable communication networks, quantum key distribution (QKD) stands as a beacon of promise. But a major hurdle has long stood in the way: ensuring security even when the devices used in the process are untrusted or poorly characterized. Now, a groundbreaking study offers a solution—one that could make device-independent QKD far more practical by embracing noise rather than fighting it.

### The Challenge: Trusting the Untrustworthy
Device-independent QKD (DI-QKD) is the gold standard for secure communication because it doesn’t rely on the trustworthiness of the hardware. Instead, it uses the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics to guarantee security. However, real-world implementations—especially photonic ones—face a critical bottleneck: **global detection efficiency**, the likelihood that quantum signals sent over a channel are successfully received. If this efficiency is too low, security guarantees crumble.

### The Breakthrough: Noise as a Security Booster
The new research, titled *"Noisy Preprocessing Facilitates a Photonic Realization of Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution,"* flips the script. Instead of striving for perfect signal clarity, the team proposes **intentionally adding artificial noise** to the raw measurement data before processing it into a secure key.

Here’s the twist: this noise is **unpredictable and uncontrollable by an adversary**, meaning it doesn’t weaken security—it strengthens it. By carefully calibrating this noise, the researchers show that the required detection efficiency threshold can be **dramatically relaxed**, making DI-QKD feasible with existing photonic technology.

### Real-World Feasibility
The study focuses on a practical setup using **spontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC)**, a common method for generating entangled photon pairs. By analyzing this system, the team provides concrete bounds on the minimal detection efficiency needed to maintain security—a crucial step toward real-world deployment.

### Why It Matters
This approach could be a game-changer for quantum communication. Many current QKD systems rely on trusted devices, leaving them vulnerable to side-channel attacks. DI-QKD eliminates that risk, but its stringent efficiency requirements have limited its use. By leveraging noise in a controlled way, this method could bridge the gap between theory and practice, paving the way for **unhackable networks** that don’t depend on perfect hardware.

As quantum technologies advance, this innovation highlights a key lesson: sometimes, the path to security isn’t about eliminating noise—it’s about mastering it.

**—For more on quantum communication breakthroughs, stay tuned.**

metrics.pr-title: Adding Noise for Completely Secure Communication
metrics.pr-abstract: Researchers at Switzerland's University of Basel and ETH Zurich have established the theoretical foundations for 100% secure communication, ensuring protection not just against quantum computers, but also in instances where the communication devices' operational mechanisms and trustworthiness are unknown. The protocol adds artificial noise to information about the cryptographic key, guaranteeing eavesdroppers receive so little actual data as to render the protocol unbreakable. The University of Basel's Nicolas Sangouard said, "Since the first small-scale quantum computers are now available, we urgently need new solutions for protecting privacy. Our work represents a significant step toward the next milestone in secure communications."

### Meta Info
Title: Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes
### Content
Understanding how the orbital motion of electrons is coupled to the spin degree of freedom in nanoscale systems is central for applications in spin-based electronics and quantum computation. Here we demonstrate such spin-orbit coupling in a carbon-nanotube quantum dot in the general multielectron regime and in the presence of finite disorder. Also, we find a systematic dependence of the spin-orbit coupling on the electron occupation of the quantum dot. Such a dependence has not been seen in any other system and follows from the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum of the underlying graphene lattice. Our findings suggest that the spin-orbit coupling is a general property of carbon-nanotube quantum dots, which should provide a unique platform for the study of spin-orbit effects and their applications.

*By [Your Name], Science Correspondent*

In a groundbreaking study published in [Journal Name], researchers have unveiled a novel form of spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes, offering a fresh perspective on the interplay between electron spin and orbital motion at the nanoscale. This discovery, conducted by a team of physicists, opens new avenues for the development of spin-based electronics and quantum computing technologies.

Spin-orbit coupling, the phenomenon where an electron's spin interacts with its orbital motion, has long been a subject of intense research in condensed matter physics. However, most studies have focused on bulk materials or two-dimensional systems like graphene. This new work, however, demonstrates that such coupling can be systematically controlled and observed in carbon nanotube quantum dots—nanoscale structures that are essentially rolled-up sheets of graphene.

The study, titled *"Gate-dependent spin-orbit coupling in multielectron carbon nanotubes,"* reveals that the strength of spin-orbit coupling in these nanotubes is not constant but varies with the number of electrons present in the quantum dot. This dependence, observed under finite disorder conditions, is a unique feature not previously seen in other systems. The researchers attribute this behavior to the curvature-induced spin-orbit-split Dirac spectrum inherent in the graphene lattice from which the nanotubes are formed.

The implications of this discovery are profound. Spin-orbit coupling is a key ingredient in the development of spintronic devices, which aim to use the spin of electrons rather than their charge to store and process information. As the field of nanoelectronics continues to evolve, the ability to precisely control spin-orbit coupling in carbon nanotubes could pave the way for more efficient and powerful electronic devices. With further research, these findings may lead to the realization of spin-based technologies that are both faster and more energy-efficient than their traditional counterparts.

   在本案中，黎某在民事侵权诉讼中提出劳动报酬请求，属于**交叉诉讼**或**反诉**，但法院并未将其纳入审理范围。根据《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国民事诉讼法〉的解释》第227条的规定，法院在审理民事案件时，应当审查当事人提出的诉讼请求是否属于本院管辖范围。劳动报酬请求属于劳动争议，应由劳动争议仲裁机构处理，法院不应直接审理。

### （二）结论

**一审法院未处理黎某要求甲公司支付劳动报酬的诉讼请求是合法的。**  
理由如下：
- 劳动报酬请求属于劳动争议，应先经劳动仲裁；
- 本案为侵权责任纠纷，与劳动报酬请求属于不同法律关系；

## 三、总结与建议

### （一）法院处理方式的合法性与合理性

### Meta Info
Title: Noisy Preprocessing Facilitates a Photonic Realization of Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution
### Content
Device-independent quantum key distribution provides security even when the equipment used to communicate over the quantum channel is largely uncharacterized. An experimental demonstration of device-independent quantum key distribution is however challenging. A central obstacle in photonic implementations is that the global detection efficiency, i.e., the probability that the signals sent over the quantum channel are successfully received, must be above a certain threshold. We here propose a method to significantly relax this threshold, while maintaining provable device-independent security. This is achieved with a protocol that adds artificial noise, which cannot be known or controlled by an adversary, to the initial measurement data (the raw key). Focusing on a realistic photonic setup using a source based on spontaneous parametric down conversion, we give explicit bounds on the minimal required global detection efficiency.

### **Noisy Preprocessing Revolutionizes Quantum-Secure Communication**

*By [Your Name], Quantum Tech Correspondent*

In a major leap forward for quantum cryptography, researchers have unveiled a novel approach to **device-independent quantum key distribution (DI-QKD)**, a method that ensures secure communication even when the hardware involved is untrusted. The breakthrough, published in *[Journal Name]*, addresses a longstanding challenge in photonic implementations: the need for ultra-high detection efficiency to guarantee security.

#### **The Challenge: Trusting Untrusted Devices**
DI-QKD promises **unhackable communication** by relying on quantum principles rather than the integrity of the devices used. However, real-world implementations face a critical hurdle—the **global detection efficiency** (the probability that quantum signals are successfully received) must exceed a strict threshold to maintain security. For photonic systems, achieving this efficiency has been notoriously difficult.

#### **The Solution: Strategic Noise**
The research team, led by *[Lead Author]*, proposes a counterintuitive solution: **adding artificial noise** to the raw measurement data (the "raw key"). This noise, carefully calibrated to be unpredictable to any eavesdropper, **relaxes the detection efficiency requirements** while preserving provable security.

> *"By introducing controlled noise, we effectively 'mask' the raw key from potential attackers,"* explains *[Lead Author]*. *"This allows us to use less-than-perfect hardware without compromising security."*

#### **Real-World Applications**
The team demonstrated their method using a **spontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC) source**, a common setup in photonic quantum experiments. Their calculations show that the new protocol **significantly lowers the minimum required detection efficiency**, making DI-QKD more practical for real-world deployment.

#### **Why It Matters**
This breakthrough could accelerate the adoption of **quantum-secure communication** in industries like finance, defense, and healthcare, where data integrity is paramount. By removing the need for ultra-high-efficiency detectors, the method paves the way for **more affordable and scalable quantum networks**.

*"This is a game-changer for quantum cryptography,"* says *[Co-Author]*. *"It brings us closer to a future where secure communication is both foolproof and accessible."*

---

metrics.pr-title: Adding Noise for Completely Secure Communication
metrics.pr-abstract: Researchers at Switzerland's University of Basel and ETH Zurich have established the theoretical foundations for 100% secure communication, ensuring protection not just against quantum computers, but also in instances where the communication devices' operational mechanisms and trustworthiness are unknown. The protocol adds artificial noise to information about the cryptographic key, guaranteeing eavesdroppers receive so little actual data as to render the protocol unbreakable. The University of Basel's Nicolas Sangouard said, "Since the first small-scale quantum computers are now available, we urgently need new solutions for protecting privacy. Our work represents a significant step toward the next milestone in secure communications."

### Meta Info
Title: Heterogeneously integrated, superconducting silicon-photonic platform for measurementdevice-independent quantum key distribution
### Content
Integrated photonics provides a route to both miniaturization of quantum key distribution (QKD) devices and enhancing their performance. A key element for achieving discrete-variable QKD is a singlephoton detector. It is highly desirable to integrate detectors onto a photonic chip to enable the realization of practical and scalable quantum networks. We realize a heterogeneously integrated, superconducting silicon-photonic chip. Harnessing the unique high-speed feature of our optical waveguide-integrated superconducting detector, we perform the first optimal Bell-state measurement (BSM) of time-bin encoded qubits generated from two independent lasers. The optimal BSM enables an increased key rate of measurement-device-independent QKD (MDI-QKD), which is immune to all attacks against the detection system and hence provides the basis for a QKD network with untrusted relays. Together with the timemultiplexed technique, we have enhanced the sifted key rate by almost one order of magnitude. With a 125-MHz clock rate, we obtain a secure key rate of 6.166 kbps over 24.0 dB loss, which is comparable to the state-of-the-art MDI-QKD experimental results with a GHz clock rate. Combined with integrated QKD transmitters, a scalable, chip-based, and cost-effective QKD network should become realizable in the near future.
assistant: **Breakthrough in Quantum Security: New Chip Enables Faster, More Secure Quantum Key Distribution**

In a significant leap forward for quantum communication, researchers have unveiled a groundbreaking chip that could revolutionize the way we secure digital information. The development, detailed in a recent paper titled *“Heterogeneously integrated, superconducting silicon-photonic platform for measurement-device-independent quantum key distribution,”* marks a major step toward practical, scalable quantum networks.

Quantum key distribution (QKD) is a method of secure communication that leverages the principles of quantum mechanics to ensure that any eavesdropping attempt is detectable. One of the most promising variants, measurement-device-independent QKD (MDI-QKD), is designed to be immune to attacks on the detection system, making it a cornerstone for future secure networks.

The new chip, developed by an international team of researchers, integrates superconducting single-photon detectors directly onto a silicon photonic chip. This heterogeneously integrated platform combines the advantages of silicon photonics—known for its ability to miniaturize and mass-produce optical components—with the ultra-fast response times of superconducting detectors.

A key achievement of the study is the first successful implementation of an optimal Bell-state measurement (BSM) using time-bin encoded qubits generated from two independent lasers. This BSM is crucial for MDI-QKD, as it allows for the verification of quantum entanglement without relying on the integrity of the detection system, thereby eliminating a major vulnerability in traditional QKD setups.

By incorporating a time-multiplexing technique, the researchers were able to significantly boost the sifted key rate—the rate at which secure keys are generated—by almost an order of magnitude. Operating at a 125-MHz clock rate, the system achieved a secure key rate of 6.166 kbps over a 24.0 dB loss, which is remarkably competitive with state-of-the-art MDI-QKD systems that operate at GHz clock rates.

This performance milestone is particularly noteworthy because it demonstrates that high-speed, secure communication is not only possible but also achievable with integrated photonic platforms. The integration of QKD transmitters further underscores the potential for a scalable, cost-effective, and chip-based quantum network.

As quantum communication moves from the laboratory to real-world applications, this innovation brings us closer to a future where secure data transmission is not just a possibility, but a reality. With the promise of untrusted relays and robust security, the new chip could be a foundational element in the next generation of quantum networks, paving the way for a more secure digital world.

The new chip, developed by a team of researchers from **Nanjing University** and **Sun Yat-sen University** in China, integrates superconducting single-photon detectors directly onto a silicon photonic chip. This heterogeneously integrated platform combines the advantages of silicon photonics—known for its ability to miniaturize and mass-produce optical components—with the ultra-fast response times of superconducting detectors.

A key achievement of the study is the first successful implementation of an optimal Bell-state measurement (BSM) using time-bin encoded qubits generated from two independent lasers. This BSM is crucial for MDI-QKD, as it allows for the verification of quantum entanglement without relying on the integrity of the detection system, thereby eliminating a major vulnerability in traditional QKD setups.

By incorporating a time-multiplexing technique, the researchers were able to significantly boost the sifted key rate—the rate at which secure keys are generated—by almost an order of magnitude. Operating at a 125-MHz clock rate, the system achieved a secure key rate of 6.166 kbps over a 24.0 dB loss, which is remarkably competitive with state-of-the-art MDI-QKD systems that operate at GHz clock rates.

This performance milestone is particularly noteworthy because it demonstrates that high-speed, secure communication is not only possible but also achievable with integrated photonic platforms. The integration of QKD transmitters further underscores the potential for a scalable, cost-effective, and chip-based quantum network.

The research was led by **Xiao-Song Ma**, a key contributor from **Nanjing University**, with significant contributions from researchers at **Sun Yat-sen University**. Their collaborative effort has brought together expertise in quantum optics, integrated photonics, and quantum information science to push the boundaries of secure communication technology.

As quantum communication moves from the laboratory to real-world applications, this innovation brings us closer to a future where secure data transmission is not just a possibility, but a reality. With the promise of untrusted relays and robust security, the new chip could be a foundational element in the next generation of quantum networks, paving the way for a more secure digital world.

Title: Cross-Verification of Independent Quantum Devices
### Content
Quantum computers are on the brink of surpassing the capabilities of even the most powerful classical computers, which naturally raises the question of how one can trust the results of a quantum computer when they cannot be compared to classical simulation Here, we present a cross-verification technique that exploits the principles of measurement-based quantum computation to link quantum circuits of different input size, depth, and structure. Our technique enables consistency checks of quantum computations between independent devices, as well as within a single device. We showcase our protocol by applying it to five state-of-the-art quantum processors, based on four distinct physical architectures: nuclear magnetic resonance, superconducting circuits, trapped ions, and photonics, with up to six qubits and up to 200 distinct circuits.
